protected_paths.description: Files and directories that require governance-class approval to modify. Any pull request touching these paths without governance_steward authorization and a governance-class approval label must be blocked.

governance_only_paths.description: Subset of protected paths that are constitutional — foundational governance documents. These define the rules that all other rules derive from. Modification requires constitutional amendment process with full governance_steward review and impact assessment.

role_limited_paths.description: Paths that may only be modified by specific roles as part of their authorized workflow. Other roles must not touch these files even if they appear in a task contract scope.

mappings.reason: Policy files are governance assets requiring governance_steward authority.
Changelog entries are produced by builders and reviewed by release managers.
Workflow definitions affect process enforcement and require planning or governance authority.
Wiki content is maintained by builders and audited by documentation managers.
Release notes are exclusively managed by the release manager role.

approval_required_paths.description: Paths that require a specific minimum approval class on the pull request before merge is permitted.

mappings.reason: Constitutional document requires full governance review.
All policy manifests require governance-class approval.
Governance enforcement workflows are protected at governance level.
CODEOWNERS changes affect review routing and require elevated approval.
Source code changes require at minimum standard approval.
Documentation changes require at minimum standard approval.

notes: Path patterns use glob syntax. ** matches any depth of directories.
When a file matches multiple mappings, the most restrictive rule applies.
Protected path enforcement is validated by compliance rule FAE-C004.
Governance isolation is validated by compliance rule FAE-C010.
Role-limited path enforcement is validated by the role_action_audit workflow referenced in FAE-C003.
Emergency approval class may override standard and sensitive approval requirements but never governance-class requirements.
